satire_attempt: Open-source maintainer announces deprecation with a heartfelt thread quoting Kaṭha Upaniṣad 1.2.19 in romanized Sanskrit. Original author replies with three Twitter polls and a Substack post titled 'They Killed My Module And All I Got Was Enlightenment.' Industry insiders predict a 2027 talk circuit on 'spiritual deprecation calendars.'
verdict_reason: Mocking the verse requires inventing the claim that deprecation announcements should be Sanskrit-quoting events — which the analog does not claim — or that the verse confers spiritual benefit on deprecators — which the analog explicitly disclaims via the falsifiability ('the verse does not absolve the deprecating engineer of communication, migration paths, or downstream care'). The verse is doctrinally about a category mistake; my satire conflates the category-mistake claim with a self-help claim. Strawman. PASS.
